articles.title: People thought this MLB hat had a defect. It’s actually a clever hidden reference
articles.summary: An exclusive Phillies All-Star Game hat features a graphic that history buffs will recognize.

articles.title: Studio Libeskind Designs New High-Density Residential Towers in Seoul
articles.summary: Studio Libeskind , in collaboration with HJ Design Partners and Seoul-based SAMOO Architects & Engineers , has designed a new high-density residential development in Seoul , South Korea . The Daechi Ssangyong 1 Redevelopment Project replaces an existing five-building, 630-unit complex in Daechi-dong, Gangnam-gu, with six new residential towers up to 49 storeys tall and nearly 1,000 housing units. The project is being developed for the Daechi Ssangyong 1 Redevelopment Union by Samsung C&T, with construction expected to begin in 2027 and be completed in 2030.

articles.title: Beyond the Canopy: Six Architectural Strategies for Urban Shade
articles.summary: As cities around the world brace for extreme temperatures and waves of unrelenting heat, urban greening is one of the best solutions for leaders to combat this reality. The benefits of planting trees for physical health, mental well-being, and thermal relief are well documented, and a mature canopy can transform a space by several degrees on a hot day. Trees cool the air through evapotranspiration, filter pollutants and absorb stormwater, and enhance biodiversity in urban spaces.

articles.title: Casa Jar / Estudio Nada
articles.summary: Casa JAR is an introspective house designed by Estudio Nada and Crux Arquitectura, where a succession of programmatic "cubes" embraces a large central courtyard that acts as a green lung and social core.

articles.title: Drifting - Fusuma-e House and Studio / knof
articles.summary: An apartment overlooking both century-old trees and the skyline of central Tokyo—we designed this residence as the Japanese home base for a music producer and DJ. For a client whose life is shaped by migration—constantly moving across the world for live performances and music production—we sought to create one of the most relaxing places on Earth. Our aim was to create a space with a profound sense of depth: one that, despite its location in the heart of the city, remains connected to nature, to light and shadow, to art and craftsmanship—to beauty in all its forms.

articles.title: How a New Generation Is Designing at the Confluence of Cultures
articles.summary: A profound generational evolution is rewriting the rules of the contemporary landscape, bringing an end to the era where architects and designers merely mimicked the tenets of Western modernism. Recorded live at Milan Design Week 2026 in cooperation with INDX|GLOBAL , the seventh episode of the Room for Dreams podcast leads an insightful session on how a new generation of Indian practitioners is stepping onto the global stage with a confident, self-aware perspective.

articles.title: Shanghai Qiyuan Experimental Primary School / Atelier Archmixing
articles.summary: Shanghai Qiyuan Experimental Primary School is the only chess-specialized school in Shanghai . With its growth, the original campus could no longer meet functional needs. The Jing'an District government decided to build a new campus during the urban renewal of Suhewan area. The site is bordered by a mature residential community to the north and Xiangdong Middle School to the east, while the west and south are both plots for future development.

articles.title: L.A. House / Emedos Proyectos
articles.summary: Located in the gated community of La Z Residence, in the central area of Jarabacoa, Dominican Republic, Casa L.A. seeks to establish a symbiosis between the physical context of the site and the architectural work, while internally integrating the home's social spaces.

articles.title: Xangri-Lá Residence / Yan Kruchin_
articles.summary: The project stems from the clients' functional need to reorganize the layout of their existing beach house, which originally had all bedrooms on the upper floor. The requirement for a ground-floor suite—more accessible and integrated into the couple's daily routine—opened the way for an intervention that reinterprets the logic of the existing structure.

articles.title: Naab House / Di Frenna Arquitectos
articles.summary: The first impression of the house does not come from its details, but from its mass. A restrained composition of clean lines, where horizontality dominates, is interrupted only by a central volume that hosts the entrance. This is not an isolated gesture, but rather a pause: a point of tension that organizes the facade and anticipates the interior experience.

articles.title: Roots House / Studio Gontijo Arquitetura
articles.summary: Located atop a cliff in Trancoso , in southern Bahia, Casa Raízes was conceived through a deep relationship with its territory. Offering permanent views of the horizon and the ocean, the site features two defining characteristics for the design: the lush presence of the Atlantic Forest and immediate proximity to the sea, accessed via an exclusive trail through native vegetation to Itapororoca Beach. The privileged location of the plot, combined with its proximity to another family property, was decisive in its selection.

articles.title: Wabamun Lake Cabin  / Josh Kjenner
articles.summary: Situated on Wabamun Lake, Josh Kjenner Architect completes a contemporary cabin which offers a retreat for a young family an hour just outside of Edmonton. The homeowners—an academic and an entrepreneur—grew up spending summers at Alberta lake cabins and envisioned this as a place to recreate those memories for their two young children where kayaking, skating, hockey and tobogganing unfold through the seasons.

articles.title: Roberts Gray Architects tops New Zealand sand dune with rammed-earth home
articles.summary: New Zealand studio Roberts Gray Architects has completed a coastal home near Aukland, wrapping two contrasting courtyards with low-slung volumes of rammed earth. Named Double Courtyard House, the home sits atop a sand dune in Te Arai that is hugged by a pine forest to one side and faces expansive coastal views to the other. The post Roberts Gray Architects tops New Zealand sand dune with rammed-earth home appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Studio Fuksas-designed Rike Concert Hall in Tbilisi set to be demolished
articles.summary: The unopened Rike Concert Hall in Tbilisi, designed by Italian practice Studio Fuksas, is set to be demolished less than 15 years after it was completed. Tbilisi's city hall architecture department has issued a permit allowing the owner of the concert hall to demolish the building. According to reports in the Georgian press, its owners The post Studio Fuksas-designed Rike Concert Hall in Tbilisi set to be demolished appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Studio Libeskind designs cluster of Seoul skyscrapers as "a living work of art"
articles.summary: Architecture firm Studio Libeskind has unveiled designs for six skyscrapers set to form a residential complex in Seoul's Gangnam district. Studio Libeskind collaborated with local architecture studios Samoo and HJ Design Partners on the design for the Daechi Ssangyong redevelopment project, which aims to connect residents with the landscape. The development is set to be built The post Studio Libeskind designs cluster of Seoul skyscrapers as "a living work of art" appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: IKEA releases portable furniture designed to be moved without a car
articles.summary: Swedish brand IKEA has unveiled its Kompishäng collection for short-term renters, featuring a £7 wardrobe organiser that doubles as a backpack and a side table that can be carried over the arm like a handbag. Kompishäng is a 12-piece range of "affordable and portable" furniture and homeware, none of which costs more than £70. The The post IKEA releases portable furniture designed to be moved without a car appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Delcy Morelos creates 30-tonne earthen installation informed by a mother's womb for the Barbican
articles.summary: Colombian artist Delcy Morelos discusses how earth, darkness and the qualities of a mother's womb informed her installation for the Barbican Centre in London in this exclusive video produced by Dezeen. The 24-metre-wide installation, named Origo, was built by hand using more than 30 tonnes of soil and clay. It takes the form of an The post Delcy Morelos creates 30-tonne earthen installation informed by a mother's womb for the Barbican appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Robert Wun adorns couture gown with kaleidoscope of colourful balloons
articles.summary: Clusters of balloons protruded from avant-garde garments at London designer Robert Wun's Autumn Winter 2026 haute couture show, giving the impression of inflatable-laden street vendors at a funfair. Hong Kong-born Wun presented his Childsplay collection during Couture Week in Paris. "The collection unfolds as a timeline of growing up," explained the designer, who unveiled 25 The post Robert Wun adorns couture gown with kaleidoscope of colourful balloons appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Panasonic launches Japanese Microwave designed for modern American kitchens
articles.summary: Promotion: electronics brand Panasonic has launched a minimalist microwave that seeks to bring Japanese design principles into North American homes. The new Panasonic Japanese Countertop Microwave NN-SF57RM is billed as marrying the understated design and precision engineering that Japan is known for. It has a streamlined profile with softly rounded contours, a simple interface and The post Panasonic launches Japanese Microwave designed for modern American kitchens appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Colorado house by Studio B and Studio Brother John appears to "hover over the land"
articles.summary: Local outfit Studio Brother John has collaborated with Studio B Architecture + Interiors for a house in Boulder, Colorado, USA making the most of its mountainous site and replacing a structure destroyed in a wildfire. Local interior designer and developer John Canova of Studio Brother John collaborated with Studio B Architecture + Interiors (Studio B), an The post Colorado house by Studio B and Studio Brother John appears to "hover over the land" appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.summary: The latest Dezeen Debate newsletter features the transformation of a historic Spanish bank into the Faro Santander museum by David Chipperfield Architects. Subscribe to Dezeen Debate now. Scheduled to open in September, the museum will open up the listed Pereda Building on the Santander seafront to the public for the first time. Commenters weren't to The post Dezeen debate features "refuge for the uncreative" appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Play-Doh enters "home decor space" with flower-making kit for adults
articles.summary: Toy company Hasbro has announced a line of Play-Doh products for adults with the release of Blooms, a flower-making kit for creating "lifelike flowers" that can be coated with a finishing spray for preservation. The Blooms kits contain tools such as rollers to press patterns into the Play-Doh, a hand-press to make moulds, and a vase The post Play-Doh enters "home decor space" with flower-making kit for adults appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Heavily stylistic approach to stadium design "should die down" says Populous global director Jonathan Mallie
articles.summary: Continuing our Future Stadium series, we interview architect Jonathan Mallie about the combination of urban planning, computational design and economics behind Populous, the studio driving global stadium design. If you attend professional sporting events, there's a good chance you've sat in a stadium designed by Populous. The Kansas City-based architecture studio, which operates globally, claims The post Heavily stylistic approach to stadium design "should die down" says Populous global director Jonathan Mallie appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Essence tiles by Casalgrande Padana
articles.summary: Dezeen Showroom: Italian brand Casalgrande Padana has created an ode to larch wood with its Essence porcelain stoneware tiles, part of the Elements collection. Essence tiles combine the rich grain and rustic character of larch – a timber often used in the construction and decoration of alpine lodges – with the high performance characteristics of The post Essence tiles by Casalgrande Padana appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.summary: A deep midnight anodized finish joins the Original collection permanently, extending across aluminum luggage, leather bags, and travel accessories.

articles.title: please touch the art: takako saito turned spectators into players
articles.summary: decades before museums embraced participation and 'user experience', the late japanese-born artist built a practice grounded in material curiosity and joy. The post please touch the art: takako saito turned spectators into players appeared first on designboom | architecture & design magazine .

articles.title: this surfboard doubles as a 10-liter beer tap with a built-in pressure chamber
articles.summary: A collaboration between surfboard shaper Chris Garrett and brewing company Gage Roads Brew Co. produced 'beerboard', a surfboard that functions as a 10‑liter beer tap with an internal pressure chamber. The design merges recreational surfing with a portable beverage dispensing system. It showcases playful engineering and cross‑industry creativity.

articles.title: belinda stewart architects to lead preservation of frank lloyd wright’s ‘fountainhead’ house
articles.summary: Belinda Stewart Architects will lead the preservation of Frank Lloyd Wright’s only Mississippi residence, the ‘Fountainhead’ house. The project aims to convert the building into a publicly accessible house museum slated to open in 2028. This effort preserves a unique piece of architectural heritage for future generations.

articles.title: a garden-within-a-garden unfolds through water, translucent pavilions, and green courtyards
articles.summary: Yanlord Arcadia transforms a residential courtyard into a layered garden landscape featuring water elements, translucent pavilions, and green courtyards. The design creates a micro‑sustainable oasis that blurs the boundary between indoor and outdoor spaces. The project showcases contemporary landscape architecture with a focus on visual layering and ecological integration.

articles.title: koala kare expands accessibility by designing for assisted care in public
articles.summary: Koala Kare introduces an adult changing station designed to prioritize dignity and support caregivers in public settings. The layout includes accessible features such as clear signage and adequate space for assistance. This project addresses a societal need for inclusive public facilities.

articles.title: father-daughter duo transforms surfboard waste into furniture and everyday objects
articles.summary: A father‑daughter team repurposes surfboard production waste into functional furniture and everyday objects. The project demonstrates a circular design approach that reduces waste and adds aesthetic value. Their work highlights innovative reuse of marine materials in product design.

articles.title: five intersecting cylinders shape fran silvestre arquitectos’ house in madrid
articles.summary: Fran Silvestre Arquitectos design a house in Madrid using five intersecting cylinders that connect through orthogonal junctions Jest ~ generating fluid transitions between rooms. The result is a dynamic spatial organization that challenges conventional rectangular layouts. The project exemplifies contemporary architectural experimentation with form and volume.

articles.title: a terraced timber pavilion explores circular construction for austria’s gabonsa music festival
articles.summary: Students from Kaiserslautern University of Applied Sciences design a terraced timber pavilion for Austria’s Gabonsa music festival. The structure employs circular construction techniques to create an engaging, modular stage environment. The project showcases innovative use of timber and circular design principles in temporary architecture.

articles.title: Decibel's Circular-Material 3D Printed Chair
articles.summary: Decibel’s chair appears wicker‑like but is actually 3‑D‑printed from Filaflex, a bio‑based, compostable filament. The design highlights sustainable material innovation and the potential of additive manufacturing. The chair combines circular economy principles with contemporary aesthetic trends.

articles.title: Furniture for Committed Book Lovers
articles.summary: Fernanda Díaz Tijerina’s Totem series offers furniture specifically tailored for avid readers, available in stainless steel, wood, and stone. The designs aim to create dedicated, functional reading spaces that.interact with the user. The collection reflects a thoughtful approach to combining material variety and user needs in furniture design.

articles.title: Designs for Modular Homes that Disappear Into the Ground During Natural Disasters
articles.summary: HiberTec Homes proposes a modular house that acts as a giant elevator, disconnecting utilities and sinking from the ground during disasters. The retractable titanium alloy lid seals the structure, aiming to protect residents from extreme events like wildfires. The concept represents an innovative intersection of disaster resilience and modular housing.
